parts: I want to plan a weekend hike somewhere within two hours of the city. Conditions matter — prefer a shaded route under 10km with a view at the top.

Three solid candidates: Cedar Ridge (8km loop, eastern exposure), Hollow Creek (6km out-and-back, riverside shade), and Painted Bluff (9km point-to-point, panoramic summit but exposed last kilometre). Given your shade preference, Hollow Creek is the safest pick; Cedar Ridge is the best all-rounder.
Book me on the Cedar Ridge option. Saturday morning, early start.
Cedar Ridge trailhead is drop-in only (no booking). Leaving the city by 06:30 puts you on the trail by 08:00 with comfortable heat margin. I'll add a reminder and pack list.
Remind me Friday night to prep water, snacks, and sun hat.
